creator: jaco@videoafrica.co.za
text:
After loading FreeBSD's 7.0 i386 install CD and entering 'sysinstall', a Standard Install does not detect any hard disks. Both hard disks in my machine is SATA, and I believe the problem does not occur with PATA disks (even though I do not have one to test). The SATA hard drives run off a SiS 180/181 RAID Controller (even though they are not in RAID mode, and there is no ATA support in the BIOS) which is part of the Asus P4S-800D series. During boot time, I can see the device being loaded/probed.

A similar ticket has been opened in the past: kern/87585, however I was not around then to test it.

Fix: 

There are a few references, one of them is a ticket opened in the past: kern/87585

A similar problem cropped into NetBSD, one (possible) solution was:
http://archive.netbsd.se/?ml=netbsd-tech-kern&a=2004-11&m=494701
How-To-Repeat: Simply insert any 6.3 or 7.0 FreeBSD install disk into a machine with only SATA hard drives. Load into sysinstall and do a standard install.
